briefing.opening: Good morning. Welcome to the climate briefing for June 15, 2026. Today, both globally and domestically, various efforts to address climate change have been reported, alongside warnings highlighting its severity.

sections.title: 🔴 Deepening Climate Crisis Warnings and Global Impacts
sections.content: Global warming reached 1.37°C in 2025, showing accelerated heat accumulation [116]. Extreme heat of 42°C has already occurred in France, leading to emergency situations across Europe and exacerbating difficulties in workplaces due to the unseasonably early heat [22, 111]. Regional impacts are intensifying due to climate change, with decreasing oxygen levels in rivers worldwide [124], a reshaping of rockfall risks in Spain [115], and heavy, unabsorbable rainfall in Maine, USA [117]. Furthermore, the risk of heatwaves has increased in nearly half of World Cup matches [119], and warnings have been raised that damage to northern peatlands and an increase in yellow dust occurrences could further accelerate climate change [126, 129].

sections.title: 🌱 Domestic and International Climate Change Adaptation and Carbon Neutrality Practices
sections.content: Mongolia, at the forefront of the climate crisis, is opening a new chapter through 'tree planting' [1, 14]. Domestically, adaptation efforts in the agricultural sector are active, with the Jeonnam Agricultural Research and Extension Services initiating the selection of domestic potato varieties resilient to climate change [3]. Yangyang County is successfully implementing customized support projects for mushrooms and cherries in response to rising material costs and climate change [6, 18, 21]. Cheonan City is accelerating its climate crisis response by improving welfare centers for the elderly and strengthening flood prevention [13]. Various local governments and institutions are encouraging citizen participation through diverse campaigns and projects such as carbon neutrality point schemes [23, 41, 48, 72, 84, 85, 86], Eco-Sharing Markets [25], carbon reduction challenges [28, 44, 49, 53, 65, 82, 83, 88], and plogging [52, 81] [27, 31, 40, 42, 45, 47, 56, 58, 59, 63, 64, 66, 68, 70, 71, 73, 75, 77, 79, 87, 89, 91]. Notably, Chungnam Province is smoothly advancing a project to transform Hongseong forests into future-oriented carbon-neutral model forests [29, 33]. Furthermore, a research team from Gongju National University has shown positive research outcomes by continuously publishing carbon-neutral solutions through tidal flat blue carbon research in international academic journals [61, 62, 67].

sections.title: 🇰🇷 Accelerating Energy Transition and Current Challenges
sections.content: Domestically, energy transition policies are being strengthened, including the promotion of electricity tariff reductions for hydrogen-reduced ironmaking to support the steel industry's carbon-neutral transition [35, 55], and the mandatory installation of new and renewable energy facilities in public parking lots [94]. Positive developments are observed, such as Yeosu City's plan to host the 2026 Offshore Wind Supply Chain Conference and Exhibition [15], and the opening of the Korea Power Exchange's Central Headquarters to respond to the expansion of renewable energy [98]. However, concerns have been raised that the pace of renewable energy deployment is excessively rapid [96], and that renewable energy targets overly reliant on solar power could increase the risk of large-scale blackouts [105]. With domestic solar power generation costs reaching twice the international average [101], and warnings about potential logistics disruptions due to the increasing size of wind and battery components [100], balancing the energy mix and securing a stable supply chain are emerging as critical challenges [95].

sections.title: 📰 Enhancing Understanding of Climate Change Science and Policy Recommendations
sections.content: Efforts to enhance public understanding of climate change also continue. The Korea Meteorological Administration is holding the '3rd Climate Change Science Quiz Competition' for middle school students nationwide, taking a first step towards resolving the climate crisis [2, 4, 5, 7, 9, 10, 11]. Daishin Economic Research Institute hosted a seminar on 'Carbon Neutral Investment and Transition Finance' to deepen related discussions [54], and the Emissions Trading System, a key tool for achieving carbon neutrality, is set to be significantly strengthened from 2026 to 2035 [74]. In Sejong City, the opinion was presented that digitalization is necessary for responding to the climate crisis [19], and an argument was also made to shift the paradigm of national infrastructure investment from recovery-centric to prevention-centric [8]. Professor Yoon Je-yong of Seoul National University emphasized that carbon neutrality should be viewed not merely as a cost but as an industrial transformation strategy [36], highlighting the importance of a policy-driven approach.

briefing.closing: Through today's briefing, we have confirmed the severity of the climate crisis and the diverse response efforts being made at global and domestic levels. Climate change is a critical challenge that requires everyone's cooperation and sustained attention, and we must continue to seek wise solutions based on scientific foundations. This concludes the daily climate briefing for June 15, 2026.
